paper_title: COCOA Study on Childhood Origin of Asthma and Allergic Diseases

methods.description: Process for selecting and analyzing the study population from the COCOA cohort to investigate the effects of various factors on pediatric susceptibility to allergic diseases.

steps.description: Regular follow-up visits involving physician examination and self-report questionnaires concerning the environment were conducted at at least 26 weeks gestation, at birth, 6 months, 1 year, and then annually.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct follow-up visits and collect self-report questionnaires

steps.description: From yearly follow-ups of pediatric allergists, we collected information on the allergic symptoms, diagnosis, and prescribed treatment.
steps.searchable_summary: collect allergic symptoms, diagnosis, and treatment data from pediatric allergists

steps.description: Among the 3,102 pairs enrolled in the COCOA cohort, 1,636 children were aged over 6 years. The 706 patients lacking clinical and laboratory data were excluded, leaving 930 patients to be analyzed.
steps.searchable_summary: filter cohort data to include only children over 6 years with complete clinical and laboratory data

steps.description: Significant differences in birth weight, breastfeeding rates, and antibiotic use before 1 year of age were observed between the included and excluded groups.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze differences in birth weight, breastfeeding rates, and antibiotic use

steps.description: Allergic diseases including PFAS were diagnosed by a physician or typical symptoms (itching, sore throat, or swelling) in the lips, mouth, and throat immediately after eating fresh fruits or vegetables by parental questionnaire.
steps.searchable_summary: collect PFAS diagnosis data from physician and parental questionnaires

steps.description: If PFAS was suspected, the trigger of PFAS was also investigated.
